content: Self-Refine's single-LLM iterative self-improvement loop can be fundamentally boosted by integrating multiple inconsistent self-perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a "multi-lens self-refinement" framework that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of no extra training or supervision while resolving its blind spot of being stuck in unrecognized flaws from a single self-perspective. This framework would let an LLM first generate an initial output, then pull conflicting critiques from its own inconsistent solving perspectives before iteratively refining, rather than only giving feedback from the same single frame that created the initial output.
